title: Pararistolochia flos-avis
taxonomy: Pararistolochia flos-avis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Aristolochiaceae, commonly known as the birthwort family. This plant belongs to the genus Pararistolochia, which contains several species of tropical and subtropical plants. The Aristolochiaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Americas, Asia, and parts of Africa. Pararistolochia flos-avis is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, where it grows in the understory of rainforests. The classification of Pararistolochia flos-avis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

side_effects: Pararistolochia flos-avis contains aristolochic acid, which is known to be nephrotoxic and carcinogenic. Ingestion of Pararistolochia flos-avis can also cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting. As a result, Pararistolochia flos-avis is considered a highly toxic and potentially deadly plant.
intro: Pararistolochia flos-avis, also known as the parrot's beak or bird's flower, is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and malaria, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
